ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus / Diamond Sleet — Trojanized signed binary side-loading malicious DLL from user-writable path (Operation Dream Job, 3CX, AppleJeus,
ucs.description: Hunts the DLL search-order hijack pattern that is the through-line of every major Lazarus / Diamond Sleet (ZINC) campaign of the last five years: a legitimately-signed application (3CXDesktopApp.exe, trojanized PUTTY/PSCP, AmazonVNCInterface.exe, CelasTradePro/JMTTrader/UnionCryptoTrader, CyberLink Promeo) is dropped to %TEMP%/%APPDATA%/Public/ProgramData by an ISO/IMG/ZIP job-lure or supply-chain installer and then loads a sibling malicious DLL (ffmpeg.dll, msvcr100.dll, dave.dll, colorui.dll, d3dcompiler_47.dll) which stages the second-stage RAT (ICONIC, MagicRAT, AIRDRY, LambLoad).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("3CXDesktopApp.exe","AmazonVNCInterface.exe","PUTTY.EXE","pscp.exe","plink.exe","CyberLinkPromeo.exe","CelasTradePro.exe","JMTTrader.exe","UnionCryptoTrader.exe","Kupay_Wallet.exe","Ant_Trader.exe","sumatrapdf.exe","wsmprovhost.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Desktop\\*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_path [ | tstats `summariesonly` count as DllLoads values(Filesystem.file_name) as SideloadedDll values(Filesystem.file_hash) as DllSHA256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Desktop\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash | rename Filesystem.dest as dest Filesystem.file_path as process_path] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus / Diamond Sleet — trojanized signed app side-loading malicious DLL
// Covers Operation Dream Job (PuTTY/AmazonVNC), 3CX (3CXDesktopApp+ffmpeg.dll),
// CyberLink LambLoad, AppleJeus (CelasTradePro/JMTTrader/UnionCryptoTrader/Kupay).
let LazarusLureExes = dynamic([
    "3cxdesktopapp.exe","amazonvncinterface.exe","putty.exe","pscp.exe",
    "plink.exe","cyberlinkpromeo.exe","celastradepro.exe",
    "jmttrader.exe","unioncryptotrader.exe","kupay_wallet.exe",
    "ant_trader.exe","sumatrapdf.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","dave.exe",
    "tradingtechnologies.exe"
]);
let LazarusSideloadDlls = dynamic([
    "ffmpeg.dll","d3dcompiler_47.dll","msvcr100.dll","libcurl.dll",
    "dave.dll","colorui.dll","version.dll","cryptbase.dll",
    "dbghelp.dll","mscoree.dll","wtsapi32.dll","winmm.dll",
    "secur32.dll"
]);
let SuspectPaths = dynamic([
    @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\",
    @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Desktop\", @"\Temp\"
]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
// loading EXE lives in a user-writable dir (ISO mount, mail-drop, download)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (SuspectPaths)
// either a known Lazarus lure binary OR a known Lazarus side-load DLL filename
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LazarusLureExes)
   or FileName in~ (LazarusSideloadDlls)
// the DLL is sibling to the EXE (classic side-load layout) — not a system DLL
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
   and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| extend KnownLazarusExe = InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LazarusLureExes),
         KnownLazarusDll = FileName in~ (LazarusSideloadDlls)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          LureExe       = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LureExePath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LureExeSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          LureExeCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          SideloadedDll = FileName,
          DllPath       = FolderPath,
          DllSHA256     = SHA256,
          KnownLazarusExe, KnownLazarusDll
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Lazarus / Diamond Sleet — trojanized signed app spawning from user-writable path
// Sentinel approximation of the Defender DLL side-load query — falls back to
// process-create (4688) because SecurityEvent has no DLL-load equivalent.
// For a full DLL-load match, ingest Sysmon EID 7 via WindowsEvent and adapt.
let LazarusLureExes = dynamic([
    "3CXDesktopApp.exe","AmazonVNCInterface.exe","PUTTY.EXE","pscp.exe",
    "plink.exe","CyberLinkPromeo.exe","CelasTradePro.exe",
    "JMTTrader.exe","UnionCryptoTrader.exe","Kupay_Wallet.exe",
    "Ant_Trader.exe","sumatrapdf.exe","wsmprovhost.exe"
]);
let SuspectPaths = dynamic([
    @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\",
    @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\",
    @"\Desktop\", @"\Temp\"
]);
(union isfuzzy=true
  ( SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ExeName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
    | where ExeName in~ (LazarusLureExes)
    | where NewProcessName has_any (SuspectPaths)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              LureExe=NewProcessName, LureCmd=CommandLine,
              ParentProcess=ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType,
              Source="SecurityEvent.4688"),
  ( WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
    | where EventID == 7         // ImageLoaded
    | extend Image        = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded  = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             User         = tostring(EventData.User),
             ExeName      = tostring(split(tostring(EventData.Image), @"\")[-1]),
             DllName      = tostring(split(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded), @"\")[-1])
    | where User !endswith "$"
    | where ExeName in~ (LazarusLureExes)
         or DllName in~ ("ffmpeg.dll","d3dcompiler_47.dll","msvcr100.dll",
                         "libcurl.dll","dave.dll","colorui.dll")
    | where ImageLoaded has_any (SuspectPaths)
    | where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\")
       and not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account=User,
              LureExe=Image, LureCmd=tostring(EventData.OriginalFileName),
              ParentProcess=tostring(EventData.ParentImage),
              SideloadedDll=ImageLoaded,
              Source="Sysmon.EID7"))
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus Group — Side-Loaded DLL via Trojanized Signed Binary (Operation Dream Job / 3CX / AppleJeus / LambLoad)
id: a4a9a6b3-1c2e-4d4d-9f6a-bf3b22e1ab21
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the DLL side-loading pattern shared across Lazarus / Diamond Sleet
  (ZINC / Hidden Cobra) campaigns — a legitimately-signed application is
  dropped to a user-writable path (ISO mount, %TEMP%, %APPDATA%, Public,
  ProgramData) and loads a sibling malicious DLL that stages a second-stage
  RAT (ICONIC, MagicRAT, AIRDRY, LambLoad). Lure binaries seen in the wild
  include 3CXDesktopApp.exe (3CX supply chain), trojanized PuTTY/PSCP, and
  AmazonVNCInterface.exe (Operation Dream Job), CelasTradePro / JMTTrader /
  UnionCryptoTrader / Kupay_Wallet (AppleJeus), and CyberLinkPromeo.exe
  (LambLoad).
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/3cx-software-supply-chain-compromise
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/10/18/multiple-north-korean-threat-actors-exploiting-the-teamcity-cve-2023-42793-vulnerability/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-187a
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1195.002
  - attack.g0032
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_lure_exe:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\3CXDesktopApp.exe'
      - '\AmazonVNCInterface.exe'
      - '\PUTTY.EXE'
      - '\pscp.exe'
      - '\plink.exe'
      - '\CyberLinkPromeo.exe'
      - '\CelasTradePro.exe'
      - '\JMTTrader.exe'
      - '\UnionCryptoTrader.exe'
      - '\Kupay_Wallet.exe'
      - '\Ant_Trader.exe'
      - '\sumatrapdf.exe'
  selection_lazarus_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\ffmpeg.dll'
      - '\d3dcompiler_47.dll'
      - '\msvcr100.dll'
      - '\libcurl.dll'
      - '\dave.dll'
      - '\colorui.dll'
  selection_user_writable:
    ImageLoaded|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Desktop\'
      - '\Temp\'
  filter_system_dirs:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files'
  condition: (selection_lure_exe or selection_lazarus_dll) and selection_user_writable and not filter_system_dirs
falsepositives:
  - Portable / unsupported application installations from user profile
  - Genuine 3CX deployments still running unpatched 18.12.x clients (treat as critical anyway)
level: high
ucs.rationale: These specific lure binaries + side-load DLL filename combinations are unique to documented Lazarus / Diamond Sleet (ZINC) campaigns — Mandiant's 3CX root-cause analysis pinned ICONIC stealer to 3CXDesktopApp.exe loading ffmpeg.dll; Microsoft's LambLoad reporting tied CyberLinkPromeo.exe to a side-loaded ColorUI.dll; the AppleJeus family (CelasTradePro / JMTTrader / UnionCryptoTrader / Kupay_Walle

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus / Diamond Sleet — BYOVD: known vulnerable signed driver dropped to disk for FudModule kernel rootkit
ucs.description: Hunts Lazarus's signature 'bring-your-own-vulnerable-driver' pattern — the actor drops a Microsoft- or vendor-signed but vulnerable .sys (Dell dbutil_2_3.sys CVE-2021-21551, ENE Technology RTCore64.sys CVE-2019-16098, Gigabyte gdrv.sys, AppID.sys CVE-2022-22047) to disk and loads it to disable EDR callbacks via the FudModule rootkit. This is one of the few APTs to do this in the wild post-2022 (Avast and Microsoft attribution).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND (Filesystem.file_name IN ("dbutil_2_3.sys","DBUtilDrv2.sys","RTCore64.sys","gdrv.sys","ene.sys","WnBios.sys","appid.sys","AsIO3.sys","atillk64.sys","ucorew64.sys","PROCEXP152.sys") OR (Filesystem.file_name="*.sys" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*"))) AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","TrustedInstaller.exe","TiWorker.exe","setup.exe","DellSupportAssist*.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | append [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" AND Registry.registry_value_name IN ("ImagePath","ServiceDll") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Public\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*") AND Registry.registry_value_data="*.sys" by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_key_name Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`]
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus FudModule BYOVD — vulnerable signed driver dropped + service registered
// Public references: Avast 'Lazarus and the FudModule Rootkit' (2024),
// Microsoft 'Diamond Sleet supply-chain' (Oct 2023), CISA AA22-187A.
let LazarusBYOVDFiles = dynamic([
    "dbutil_2_3.sys","dbutildrv2.sys","rtcore64.sys","gdrv.sys",
    "ene.sys","wnbios.sys","appid.sys","asio3.sys","atillk64.sys",
    "ucorew64.sys","procexp152.sys","piddrv.sys"
]);
let UserWritablePaths = dynamic([
    @"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Downloads\",
    @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\\Default\"
]);
let LegitInstallers = dynamic([
    "msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","tiworker.exe","setup.exe",
    "dellsupportassistagent.exe","sasdriverinstaller.exe"
]);
let DriverDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where (FileName in~ (LazarusBYOVDFiles))
         or (FileName endswith ".sys" and FolderPath has_any (UserWritablePaths))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (LegitInstallers)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project DriverDropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              ActorAccount=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              DroppedDriver=FileName, DriverPath=FolderPath, DriverSHA256=SHA256,
              DroppedBy=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DroppedByCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              DroppedByPath=InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
// Correlate with a service registration pointing at the same .sys within 1h —
// classic Lazarus FudModule flow: drop driver → CreateService → StartService.
let ServiceRegs =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
    | where RegistryKey has @"\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName in~ ("ImagePath","ServiceDll")
    | where RegistryValueData endswith ".sys"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (UserWritablePaths)
         or RegistryValueData has_any (LazarusBYOVDFiles)
    | project SvcRegTime=Timestamp, DeviceId,
              ServiceKey=RegistryKey, ServiceImage=RegistryValueData,
              SvcRegProc=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SvcRegCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
DriverDrops
| join kind=leftouter ServiceRegs on DeviceId
| where isnull(SvcRegTime)
     or (SvcRegTime between (DriverDropTime .. DriverDropTime + 1h)
         and ServiceImage has DroppedDriver)
| project DriverDropTime, DeviceName, ActorAccount,
          DroppedDriver, DriverPath, DriverSHA256,
          DroppedBy, DroppedByCmd,
          SvcRegTime, ServiceKey, ServiceImage, SvcRegProc, SvcRegCmd
| order by DriverDropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Lazarus FudModule BYOVD — driver drop correlated with service registration
let LazarusBYOVDFiles = dynamic([
    "dbutil_2_3.sys","dbutildrv2.sys","rtcore64.sys","gdrv.sys",
    "ene.sys","wnbios.sys","appid.sys","asio3.sys","atillk64.sys",
    "ucorew64.sys","procexp152.sys","piddrv.sys"
]);
let UserWritablePaths = dynamic([
    @"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Downloads\",
    @"\ProgramData\"
]);
let LegitInstallers = dynamic([
    "msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","tiworker.exe","setup.exe"
]);
let DriverDrops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where (TargetFileName in~ (LazarusBYOVDFiles))
         or (TargetFileName endswith ".sys" and TargetFilePath has_any (UserWritablePaths))
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ (LegitInstallers)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project DriverDropTime=TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              ActorUsername, DroppedDriver=TargetFileName,
              DriverPath=TargetFilePath, DriverSHA256=TargetFileSHA256,
              DroppedBy=ActingProcessName, DroppedByCmd=ActingProcessCommandLine;
// 4697 = service installed (System log). 7045 (System log) is the alternative.
let ServiceInstalls =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4697
    | where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
    | extend SvcImage = tolower(ServiceFileName)
    | where SvcImage endswith ".sys"
    | where SvcImage has_any (UserWritablePaths)
         or SvcImage has_any (LazarusBYOVDFiles)
    | project SvcInstallTime=TimeGenerated, DvcHostname=Computer,
              ServiceName, SvcImage, SvcStartType=ServiceStartType,
              SvcInstaller=SubjectUserName;
DriverDrops
| join kind=leftouter ServiceInstalls on DvcHostname
| where isnull(SvcInstallTime)
     or (SvcInstallTime between (DriverDropTime .. DriverDropTime + 1h)
         and SvcImage has DroppedDriver)
| project DriverDropTime, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DroppedDriver, DriverPath, DriverSHA256,
          DroppedBy, DroppedByCmd,
          SvcInstallTime, ServiceName, SvcImage, SvcInstaller
| order by DriverDropTime desc
ucs.rationale: BYOVD with a known-vulnerable signed driver dropped from a non-installer process is a vanishingly rare technique outside of three actor clusters — Lazarus/Diamond Sleet (FudModule), Scattered Spider (which prefers different drivers like RentDrv), and red-team Cobalt-Strike post-ex modules. Pairing the file-create with a Services\ImagePath registry write (or 4697/7045 service install) referring to
